title: Frozens Let it Go topples Cruel Angels Thesis
display_title: Frozens Let it Go topples Cruel Angels Thesis
wikitext:
[]: Frozen's 'Let it Go' topples Cruel Angel's Thesis             Quote Post by Dataprime » Mon Dec 01, 2014 1:02 pm And the Frozen mania continues... One of the largest Japanese karaoke companies, XING today announced the rankings for the most popular songs in each category based on its online karaoke service JOYSOUND for the year 2014, counted between January 1 and November 30. The No.1 song in the anime/tokusatsu/game category this year is the Japanese version of "Let It Go," a song from Disneys 2013 feature film Frozen, titled "Ari no Mamade" Neon Genesis Evangelion's OP theme "A Cruel Angel's Thesis," which had topped the ranking for 6 years in a row since 2008 , lost its top position for the first time. There are four songs from Frozen in the top 10. Anime/Tokusatsu/Game Song Ranking Top 20 SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Mon Dec 01, 2014 1:04 pm Kimochi warui.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Mon Dec 01, 2014 1:23 pm Sorrow wrote: Kimochi warui.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Dec 01, 2014 1:32 pm At least it's still top three-- and Cruel Angel's Thesis has lasted for a long time. Let's see how Frozen's popularity holds up over the same amount of time.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Mon Dec 01, 2014 1:51 pm .....Fuck you frozen.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Dec 01, 2014 6:42 pm Relax everybody. You just need to.... Ret it Go.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Dec 01, 2014 7:04 pm Ra@aaaaaaaaaaaaayyyyyge!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Tue Dec 02, 2014 2:22 am It seems that king's trone grown complacent and wobbly. It was about time for it to topple.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Dec 02, 2014 12:39 pm Went to Joysound in Shinjuku. They've got several Eva-themed rooms. Saw Frozen merch/adverts out front at stores multiple times, it was the only non-Japanese title I came across in this manner.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Dec 02, 2014 2:40 pm For those who haven't heard the Japanese version of Let It Go, here. Enjoy: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cvj3-MZO9Tw Singer: Takako Matsu I still feel Let It Go is somewhat an overrated/overhyped song, but you can't deny it's catchy. And the Frozen itself is really enjoyable, too. ;) Hope the sequel won't suck, as tends to happen with sequels. (Though, I enjoyed the Aladdin trilogy as a little girl...) About why it's "Anna and the Snow Queen": That was going to be the original title, but Disney changed it to "Frozen" so it'd appeal to boys, too. Not sure why Japan decided to go with the original as their title.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Tue Dec 02, 2014 2:48 pm Wait a sequel? well the idea of sequels is everything is meant to be "bigger" than the first, problem is if the subtext in frozen gets any bigger disney wont be able to show it on screen

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Tue Dec 02, 2014 2:56 pm I've heard this song for the first time in my life, and I really don't get, why this is getting more love than my beloved Zangoku.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Tue Dec 02, 2014 3:17 pm Woah! Judging from their clip of Let it Go, the Frozen dub is really good. I wonder if there are hardcore fans in Japan who insist on watching the (subtitled) American version. As for the song itself, I consider Komm Susser Tod to be the emotional opposite Let it Go. And Turn it Off from The Book of Mormon is the literal opposite. Think about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Dec 02, 2014 5:00 pm From what I know, there's not going to be a full-fledged Frozen 2-- just a small short, of the likes of Tangled Ever After.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Dec 03, 2014 8:35 pm So Frozen is big enough in Japan for platinum calendars to be released starting at 60,000Y. http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/interest/2014-12-03/japan-pure-platinum-frozen-calendar-costs-more-than-many-houses/.81714 khara needs to step up their [s:24ku4wo2]pimping[/s:24ku4wo2] merchandising efforts.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Dec 04, 2014 7:37 pm Well, at least they have smaller copies (for $133 USD. Yikes!)

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Dec 08, 2014 12:21 am 2014 Ranking: 30 Products that Made it Big in Japan Guess what #1 was?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Dec 08, 2014 2:57 am Elsa onahole?

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Mon Dec 08, 2014 2:59 am You act like that is not already a thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Mon Dec 08, 2014 5:42 am I don't really get the rampant rage that Frozen causes here. It is a pretty good film that fulfilled a demand that had been around for a long time: a genuinely good Disney original that actually finds the company's main target demographic. It is no masterpiece, but it is a pretty good film that reminded me of Disney's better days. Sailor Star Dust wrote: About why it's "Anna and the Snow Queen": That was going to be the original title, but Disney changed it to "Frozen" so it'd appeal to boys, too. Not sure why Japan decided to go with the original as their title.
